beckers-restaurant.name: Becker's Restaurant
beckers-restaurant.shortDesc: Michelin-starred restaurant run by the Becker family in Olewig (a wine village within Trier). Modern cuisine, estate wines, wine-led pairings.
beckers-restaurant.longDesc: Becker's Restaurant sits at the top tier of German Michelin-starred dining and is housed inside the family's Weinhaus Becker in Olewig. Wolfgang Becker composes modern tasting menus closely tied to the in-house wine selection. Reservations required.

schlemmereule.name: Schlemmereule
schlemmereule.shortDesc: Set in the cathedral cellar on the Domfreihof. Mediterranean accents on a regional foundation.
schlemmereule.longDesc: Schlemmereule occupies a historic vaulted cellar on the Domfreihof, the square next to the Trier Cathedral. Petra and Hubert Scheid have been cooking Mediterranean-regional cuisine at a consistently high level here for decades. Excellent wine list focused on the Mosel, with a calm atmosphere.

bagatelle.name: Bagatelle
bagatelle.shortDesc: Charming bistro with a French slant on Zuckerbergstraße.
bagatelle.longDesc: Bagatelle is Trier's top address for bistro cooking: a tight menu, carefully built sauces, and a wine recommendation for every course. Lovely summer terrace, intimate interior. Reservations recommended.

weinstube-kesselstatt.name: Weinstube Kesselstatt
weinstube-kesselstatt.shortDesc: Classic wine tavern on Liebfrauenplatz. Regional cooking, Mosel wines, and a wonderful terrace.
weinstube-kesselstatt.longDesc: Weinstube Kesselstatt inside the Palais Kesselstatt on Liebfrauenplatz is Trier at its purest: a courtyard terrace looking onto the cathedral and the Liebfrauenkirche (Church of Our Lady), a regional menu with Moselle classics, and wines from Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt. On a sunny day, the prettiest seat in town.

zum-domstein.name: Zum Domstein
zum-domstein.shortDesc: Restaurant built over a Roman wine press. Regional cuisine plus historical Roman menus.
zum-domstein.longDesc: Zum Domstein stands over an original 4th-century Roman wine press, which guests can visit in the cellar. The menu features regional dishes and, on request, a historical Roman menu reconstructed from ancient Apicius recipes. Touristy, but well done.

kartoffelkiste.name: Kartoffelkiste
kartoffelkiste.shortDesc: A Trier classic built around potato dishes. Family-friendly, generous portions, cozy.
kartoffelkiste.longDesc: Kartoffelkiste on the Hauptmarkt (the main market square) is a Trier classic for anyone who likes potatoes in every form: from potato pancakes to jacket potatoes, paired with Moselle sides. Family-friendly with fair portions.

pfeffermuehle.name: Pfeffermühle
pfeffermuehle.shortDesc: Restaurant with a modern take on Moselle cuisine in the Zurlauben fishermen's quarter.
pfeffermuehle.longDesc: Pfeffermühle on the Zurlaubener Ufer pairs a romantic riverside setting on the Moselle with modern regional cooking. A lovely terrace right by the water, a seasonal menu, and an elevated wine list. Also a good pick for a birthday dinner.

bei-joh-und-otto.name: Bei Joh und Otto
bei-joh-und-otto.shortDesc: Young restaurant with modern cuisine, a short menu, and a thoughtful wine selection.
bei-joh-und-otto.longDesc: Bei Joh und Otto represents Trier's younger high-end kitchen: a short, frequently changing menu, modern takes on classics, and a curated wine selection. The place to go if you want to experience Trier's food scene beyond the tourist drag.

weingut-reverchon.name: Weingut Reverchon
weingut-reverchon.shortDesc: Winery with a courtyard wine tavern. Estate wines plus Moselle platters, tarte flambée, and winemaker's bread.
weingut-reverchon.longDesc: Weingut Reverchon in Olewig opens its courtyard wine tavern to guests. The menu features estate wines by the glass and by the bottle, Moselle platters, tarte flambée, and house-made winemaker salads. Summer terrace and on-site wine sales.

weingut-rummel.name: Weingut Rummel
weingut-rummel.shortDesc: A classic Olewig Straußwirtschaft (seasonal tavern run by a winery). Estate wine, a simple menu, and a local atmosphere.
weingut-rummel.longDesc: Weingut Rummel is Olewig in its purest form: a Straußwirtschaft in the courtyard, estate Riesling and Elbling wines, and a menu of platters, vintner's soup, and fried potatoes. Local, honest, and fairly priced.

weinstube-palais-kesselstatt.name: Weinstube Palais Kesselstatt (Olewig)
weinstube-palais-kesselstatt.shortDesc: The wine tavern of Weingut Kesselstatt, with tastings and a small menu.
weinstube-palais-kesselstatt.longDesc: Weingut Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt offers tastings and wine-tavern service in Olewig. Riesling from Spätlese to dry, charcuterie platters, a cheese selection, and on-site wine sales. A quiet insider tip.

trierer-weinhaus.name: Trierer Weinhaus
trierer-weinhaus.shortDesc: A local classic in Olewig. Well-curated Mosel wine list and simple regional dishes.
trierer-weinhaus.longDesc: Trierer Weinhaus in Olewig is one of the older addresses in the village. The menu offers platters, schnitzel, and trout, alongside a very well-curated Mosel wine list featuring producers from the region. Classic and honest.

ristorante-losteria.name: Ristorante L'Osteria
ristorante-losteria.shortDesc: Italian chain serving oversized pizzas on the Hauptmarkt. Reliable and family-friendly.
ristorante-losteria.longDesc: L'Osteria on Trier's Hauptmarkt (the main market square) reliably turns out oversized pizzas, classic pastas, and a small wine list. Family-friendly and quick. Reservations are a good idea on weekends.

ristorante-la-bottega.name: Ristorante La Bottega
ristorante-la-bottega.shortDesc: Ambitious Italian on Glockenstraße. Seasonal menu and a good wine list.
ristorante-la-bottega.longDesc: La Bottega is Trier's ambitious Italian. House-made pasta, fresh antipasti, seasonal specialties, and a genuinely respectable Italian wine list. A small dining room, so reservations are advised.

pizzeria-donna-margherita.name: Pizzeria Donna Margherita
pizzeria-donna-margherita.shortDesc: Small pizzeria on Sichelstraße. Wood-fired Neapolitan pizza at fair prices.
pizzeria-donna-margherita.longDesc: Donna Margherita fires Neapolitan-style pizzas in a wood oven. Small, often packed, and very reasonably priced. Classics over experiments.

vapiano-trier.name: Vapiano Trier
vapiano-trier.shortDesc: Italian chain with live-cooking stations on the Viehmarkt.
vapiano-trier.longDesc: Vapiano on the Viehmarkt runs the familiar concept of live-cooking stations for pasta, pizza, and salad. Modern interior, well suited to quick lunch breaks and groups.

akito-sushi.name: Akito Sushi
akito-sushi.shortDesc: Sushi spot on Bruchhausenstraße. Fresh fish, fair prices, delivery available.
akito-sushi.longDesc: Akito Sushi is Trier's established sushi address. Classic maki, nigiri, and bowls, a well-priced lunch menu, and a thoughtfully chosen Japanese beer and sake selection in the evenings.

bambus-vietnamesisch.name: Bambus
bambus-vietnamesisch.shortDesc: Small Vietnamese restaurant. Pho, bun bo nam bo, and summer rolls.
bambus-vietnamesisch.longDesc: Bambus serves Vietnamese classics, pho, bun, summer rolls, and grilled skewers, in a relaxed setting. Fair prices, house-made sauces, and a friendly family-run feel.

saigon-thai.name: Saigon
saigon-thai.shortDesc: Thai cuisine on Brückenstraße. Curries, glass noodles, and heat to order.
saigon-thai.longDesc: Saigon cooks classic Thai: red and green curries, pad thai, and glass-noodle salads. Heat level to taste, a nice lunch menu, and a well-kept dining room.

bistro-quo-vadis.name: Bistro Quo Vadis
bistro-quo-vadis.shortDesc: Bistro with a rotating daily menu and a solid wine list on Stockplatz.
bistro-quo-vadis.longDesc: Quo Vadis is an honest bistro for lunch and relaxed evenings. A daily menu with three or four mains, always fairly priced, and a solid wine list.

lieblingsstube.name: Lieblingsstube
lieblingsstube.shortDesc: Café-bistro serving breakfast all day. Homemade cakes and a small hot menu.
lieblingsstube.longDesc: Lieblingsstube is a meeting point for locals who like a good breakfast that stretches past noon. Homemade cakes, a sandwich menu, a small hot daily menu, and a lovely atmosphere.

burgerlich.name: Burgerlich
burgerlich.shortDesc: Burger spot offering several patty options, a fries menu, and craft beer.
burgerlich.longDesc: Burgerlich serves burgers beyond the chain template: beef patties, pulled pork, and a vegetarian patty made with beans and quinoa. Several fries variations and craft beer from regional breweries.

tabula-rasa.name: Tabula Rasa
tabula-rasa.shortDesc: Young, modern restaurant with rotating menus and bold flavors.
tabula-rasa.longDesc: Tabula Rasa is one of Trier's newer restaurants with a clear modern identity: a rotating four- to six-course menu, bold flavors, and a strong natural-wine list. Reservations recommended.

weinstube-fwg.name: Weinstube Friedrich-Wilhelm-Gymnasium
weinstube-fwg.shortDesc: The foundation-run wine tavern of the FWG school. Wines from estate vineyards in a classic Trier setting.
weinstube-fwg.longDesc: The FWG wine tavern is run by the foundation of the Friedrich-Wilhelm-Gymnasium, which farms its own vineyards. Dry Rieslings, a small menu, and plenty of Olewig regulars.
